title: The Life of St. Norbert
summary: The publication-bound General Corpus source is the fully headed 2023 University of Michigan CME XML at CME/source/recent_changes/additions_2023/CME90162.xml, not the sibling legacy-wrapper copy under CME_from_OTA. Its encoded body revises Oxford Text Archive deposit 0162 by comparison with Cyril Lawrence Smetana's 1977 edition: U-M restored about 200 omitted lines, repaired evident OCR errors, and added structure and source locators. The OTA deposit was apparently captured from Smetana, whose edition transcribes Huntington Library mssHM 55, ff. 1r–59v, a presentation copy made by John Capgrave that Huntington describes as evidently autograph. Huntington publishes only five selected images, three of which show the work; the complete codex is not publicly digitized but a full reproduction may be requested through Aeon. DIMEV record 2973 (XML record-2973; IMEV/NIMEV 1805) independently identifies HM 55 as the sole known witness and cites the source edition.
review_notes: The approved CME90162 PDF explicitly selects the recent_changes XML. The sibling CME_from_OTA file has a different wrapper and header but an identical EEBO textual subtree; both file fixities are preserved without treating the sibling as a separate text revision.

The five-canvas Huntington object is a complete capture of the provider's published selection but only a partial surrogate of physical manuscript HM 55. It must not be presented as a whole-codex facsimile.

The medieval text, Smetana's modern editorial contribution, OTA and U-M encodings, Huntington photography, and DIMEV dataset have separate rights records. No rights statement is inherited between layers.
Negative availability findings are dated 2026-07-14 and do not exclude private, mediated, or newly published reproductions.

entities.label: General Corpus publication-bound source XML for CME90162
entities.description: The exact XML selected by the approved CME90162 publication artifact and consumed by this repository's conversion workflow.
notes: The XML records IDNO, IDG/@ID, and VID as CME90162 and retains OTA0162 in STC and BIBNO.
The publication override binds CME90162.pdf (SHA-256 29239dc05271b9116a2bd1d2955ab0c125259b904890fc73910812bfbf7f8f96) to this exact source file.

The sibling CME/source/CME_from_OTA/CME90162.xml has SHA-256 9ca357c98fef005484296858bca4d78a959f3ab986282b07dc1789bc748ddb18 and git blob 56100072b883c0765aaebc68d5860c88abf9bcab. Its wrapper/header differs, while the EEBO subtree is byte-identical to the publication-bound file.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME90162
entities.description: The 2023 U-M/Middle English Compendium revision of OTA 0162, checked against Smetana's print edition and supplied with chapter, line, page, and folio markup.

date_statements.label: revision change recorded in the header

notes: The item-level source statement says the OTA text was apparently OCR-derived, lacked about 200 lines, and retained visible OCR artifacts before U-M repair.
The stable U-M resolver is recorded even though automated retrieval encountered a current anti-bot response; the complete publication-bound XML remains locally verifiable.

entities.label: Oxford Text Archive deposit 0162
entities.description: The 1983 plain-text deposit titled The life of St. John Norbert, publicly supplied by Oxford and used as the input to U-M's 2023 revision.
notes: Oxford metadata credits John Capgrave as creator and P. J. Lucas as contributor, while the record title says compiled by P. J. Lucas. Lucas's exact production role remains to be clarified.

The deposit uses # for thorn and $ for yogh. It is the complete publicly supplied 1983 file, but it is not textually complete against Smetana: U-M reports about 200 omitted lines and evident OCR errors.

entities.label: Cyril Lawrence Smetana's 1977 edition of The Life of St. Norbert

bibliographic.citation: John Capgrave, The Life of St. Norbert, ed. Cyril Lawrence Smetana, Studies and Texts 40 (Toronto: Pontifical Institute of Mediaeval Studies, 1977), viii + 179 pp.
bibliographic.title: The Life of St. Norbert

notes: The edited text begins under the heading Life of Saint Norbert / MS HM 55 on p. 19 and runs through p. 155. The volume also contains introduction, comments by Peter J. Lucas, glossary, bibliography, scriptural citations, and index.
The title page and catalog establish Smetana as modern editor, despite the current XML's first AUTHOR element placing his name where a reader might mistake it for medieval authorship.

entities.label: Huntington Library mssHM 55
entities.description: A presentation copy of The Life of St. Norbert made by John Capgrave, written in England in 1440 and presented to John Wygenhale, abbot of West Dereham; Huntington describes the manuscript as evidently autograph. The work occupies ff. 1r–59v; a separate lifted former pastedown, f. i recto-verso, is a Latin breviary fragment and is not part of the work.

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.notes: The poem self-dates completion to Assumption week 1440, and the repository dates the manuscript [1440].

holding.notes: Physical consultation is restricted and requires curatorial approval and extended retrieval.

physical_description.support: Parchment; separate lifted former pastedown fragment also preserved
physical_description.extent: 59 folios for the Capgrave codex, plus separate former pastedown f. i recto-verso
physical_description.format: Codex in fifteenth-century leather over oak boards
notes: Capgrave wrote the work in secretary script, normally 35 verse lines per page, and supplied interlinear corrections in his own hand.
The opening historiated initial on f. 1r depicts Capgrave presenting the book to Abbot John Wygenhale; f. 59v ends with Capgrave's Feliciter signature and trefoil.
The reversed inner bifolium of quire 6 transposes the physical order of ff. 44 and 45.
The repository suggests production in England, possibly at the Augustinian house at Lynn; this is not treated as a secure place of composition.
Huntington calls the volume a presentation copy made by Capgrave and describes it as evidently autograph.

DIMEV identifies HM 55 as the unique witness of record-2973 and localizes its language to Norfolk through LALME/eLALME. That localization applies to the witness's language, not automatically to Capgrave's place of composition.

entities.label: Huntington HM 55 five-image published selection
entities.description: The complete provider-published five-canvas Digital Scriptorium selection: f. 1r, a spread showing ff. 32v–33r, f. 59v, the fore-edge binding, and the inside back cover. It is not a continuous or complete manuscript facsimile.
entities.reproduction_format: Five-canvas IIIF image selection cached at effective 1800-pixel width
notes: Canvases 1–3 overlap the work at f. 1r, ff. 32v–33r, and f. 59v. Canvases 4–5 document binding surfaces.
The official record explicitly describes these images as a partial scan made for Digital Scriptorium in the early 2000s.

entities.label: DIMEV record-2973 for The Life of St. Norbert
entities.description: The Digital Index of Middle English Verse record identifying Capgrave's poem, rhyme-royal form, unique HM 55 witness, and Smetana and Horstmann editions.
notes: The release XML identifier is record-2973 and the live DIMEV record number is 2973; 1805 is the IMEV/NIMEV number and must not be substituted into recID, because recID=1805 resolves to a different poem.
The dataset describes the opening as Joy and grace peace love faith and charity, the seven-line ababbcc stanza, HM 55 ff. 1–59v, and the Feliciter signature on f. 59v.

entities.label: Sarah James, Capgrave, Bokenham, and Late Medieval Saints' Lives (2013)
entities.description: A contextual scholarly chapter used for the poem's length, stanza form, completion, dedication, Latin-source characterization, and framing. It is not an upstream edition, encoding, or witness in CME90162's transmission chain.

bibliographic.citation: Sarah James, “Capgrave, Bokenham, and Late Medieval Saints' Lives,” in A Companion to Fifteenth-Century English Poetry, ed. Julia Boffey and A. S. G. Edwards (Cambridge: D. S. Brewer, 2013), 99–112, ISBN 9781843843535.
bibliographic.title: Capgrave, Bokenham, and Late Medieval Saints' Lives
bibliographic.container_title: A Companion to Fifteenth-Century English Poetry

notes: The author accepted manuscript does not identify HM 55 and is not evidence for an exact-witness assertion or for the pre-1422 composition proposal.

entities.label: Joseph A. Gribbin, The Date of John Capgrave's Life of St Norbert (2000)
entities.description: A contextual scholarly appendix on composition chronology. The publisher's public summary reports Peter J. Lucas's proposal that the work began before 1422 and was completed and dedicated in 1440; the source is not a transmission ancestor.

bibliographic.citation: Joseph A. Gribbin, “The Date of John Capgrave's Life of St Norbert,” appendix in The Premonstratensian Order in Late Medieval England (Woodbridge: Boydell & Brewer, 2000), 248–250.
bibliographic.title: The Date of John Capgrave's Life of St Norbert
bibliographic.container_title: The Premonstratensian Order in Late Medieval England

notes: The full chapter was not publicly downloadable during review; only the publisher's bibliographic record and summary were used for the chronology attribution.

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A
entities.description: Official federal guidance used only for cautious United States copyright-duration assessment of Smetana's 1977 edition; it neither describes nor transmits the medieval work.

bibliographic.citation: United States Copyright Office, Duration of Copyright, Circular 15A, revised April 2026.
bibliographic.title: Duration of Copyright

agents.name: John Capgrave
notes: Author-translator of the Middle English poem and principal scribe of the presentation manuscript that Huntington describes as evidently autograph.

agents.name: P. J. Lucas
notes: Oxford metadata identifies Lucas as contributor/ compiler of the 1983 deposit; Smetana's contents page separately credits Comments by Peter J. Lucas. The exact connection between those roles has not been verified.

agents.name: Oxford Text Archive, University of Oxford
Digital Index of Middle English Verse project

scope.notes: U-M retained the OTA text as its base but restored omitted lines, repaired evident OCR, and added structural and source-reference markup.

object.description: Edited Middle English Life of Saint Norbert

assertion.notes: The U-M header says apparently derived from an OCR'ed capture; no surviving OTA production file or explicit OCR log was found.

subject.description: The edited Life of Saint Norbert

object.description: Capgrave's poem, excluding the separate lifted breviary fragment

subject.description: All five canvases in the provider's published selection

object.description: Only the photographed textual and binding surfaces

scope.notes: The relation is partial at the physical-manuscript layer even though every canvas of the published selection is cached.

subject.description: DIMEV XML record-2973 / IMEV and NIMEV 1805

object.description: The Life of St. Norbert in its unique witness

access.access_method: Open the exact commit-pinned raw XML or inspect the publication-bound CME submodule file at the recorded repository path.
Use the persistent U-M resolver; the repository XML supplies a locally verifiable copy of the same U-M item.
notes: Automated retrieval of the resolver encountered an anti-bot response on the review date; this operational result does not make the otherwise public item a restricted resource.

access.access_method: Open the OTA handle record or download the exact plain-text bitstream.

local_copies.label: Exact public OTA 0162 plain-text deposit

work_portion.label: The Life of St. Norbert as supplied in the complete OTA deposit
locators: manuscript folio references f. 1r–59v embedded in the text
notes: Complete means the complete provider deposit, not a textually complete transcription: U-M reports that approximately 200 edition lines were absent.
The exact original preserves legacy # and $ character substitutions and OCR/keying artifacts. It is not a manuscript facsimile.

access.access_method: Use the Google Books limited preview. The title, copyright, and contents pages are visible, but no complete free edition is supplied.
notes: No preview images were cached because the provider exposes a copyrighted, limited, session-mediated preview rather than a stable complete download.

access.access_method: Borrow through Internet Archive controlled digital lending if eligible and signed in.
access.format: Restricted lending scan; PDF/JP2 derivatives are not anonymously downloadable
notes: The item metadata marks access-restricted-item true. No encrypted or account-gated derivative was cached.

access.access_method: Use the Huntington collection page, HDL object, or library catalog record.
Apply as a Huntington reader, submit an Aeon request, and obtain required curatorial approval before an onsite consultation.
access.contact: The Huntington Library reading-room and curatorial staff via Aeon
notes: The item record says RESTRICTED USE: extended retrieval; curatorial approval required. Reader eligibility and approval are separate from image-order availability.

access.access_method: Open the Huntington Digital Library object or exact IIIF Presentation 2 manifest.

local_copies.label: Complete Huntington HM 55 published five-image selection

work_portion.label: Published selection images overlapping The Life of St. Norbert
locators: manuscript f. 1r / IIIF canvas 1 / bundle image 000001
manuscript ff. 32v–33r / IIIF canvas 2 / bundle image 000002
manuscript f. 59v / IIIF canvas 3 / bundle image 000003
notes: Canvases 4–5 show the fore edge and inside back cover rather than additional work text.

The original ContentDM canvas identifiers are retained inside the cached IIIF manifest and inventory as technical locators; the work-portion links use working HDL item pages because direct canvas requests returned 404 during review.
ZIP contains the exact provider manifest, inventory, and all five published JPEGs requested at the effective 1800-pixel IIIF width profile.
Coverage is partial relative to physical HM 55: the unrepresented manuscript leaves, the separate breviary fragment, and most binding surfaces are unavailable in this object.

access.access_method: Create or use a Huntington Aeon account and submit a reproduction request for the complete physical object. Approval, feasibility, and final quotation depend on condition and restrictions.
access.contact: The Huntington Library Imaging Services through Aeon
access.format: Requested manuscript PDF scans or other quoted digital files
notes: The current price list states manuscript PDF scans at USD 1 per page for non-profit use and USD 2 per page for commercial use, with administrative charges for larger orders; fees can change and an exact quote controls.

A whole-object request should explicitly include both boards, spine, fore edge, pastedowns, every flyleaf and blank, ff. 1r–59v, the lifted former pastedown f. i recto-verso, color/scale targets, and any separate binding views. The corpus work should then be mapped narrowly to ff. 1r–59v.
Huntington states a standard turnaround of about 15 business days, with extended handling for requests over 50 images; HM 55's restriction may affect feasibility.

access.access_method: Open DIMEV record 2973; do not substitute the IMEV/NIMEV number 1805 into recID.
Open the Zenodo DOI record or download the exact DIMEV v1.0 release archive.

local_copies.label: Complete DIMEV v1.0 release archive

work_portion.label: DIMEV record for The Life of St. Norbert and linked HM 55 manuscript entry

notes: The retained archive is the complete lawful dataset release, not a work-only extraction. ZIP integrity was verified.

access.format: ZIP of XML data and documentation

local_copies.label: Sarah James 2013 author accepted manuscript

work_portion.label: Discussion of Capgrave's Life of St. Norbert

notes: Complete means the complete 17-page author accepted manuscript, not the whole published companion volume. qpdf reported a non-fatal object-count warning; all pages remained readable and extractable.

access.access_method: Use the official Cambridge Core chapter landing page and public summary.
access.format: Publisher bibliographic record and chapter summary
notes: Ordinary landing-page HTML was not cached; the skill requires caching exact file deliverables, while the chapter text itself was not publicly downloadable.

access.access_method: Use institutional access or the purchase options presented by Cambridge Core to read the complete chapter.
Download the official U.S. Copyright Office PDF.

local_copies.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A, revised April 2026
notes: Exact official bytes were retained. qpdf reported non-fatal linearization hint-table warnings; the four-page file is unencrypted and readable.

rights.component: John Capgrave's 1440 Middle English text and medieval manuscript content
rights.basis: The literary work and manuscript were created in 1440, centuries before modern United States copyright terms.
notes: This determination does not apply automatically to Smetana's 1977 editing, later encodings, modern photography, or provider platform terms.

rights.basis: The exact XML header says Oxford and consequently U-M distribute the item under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0 Unported.
notes: The repository's broader output license does not silently override this item-specific noncommercial source statement.

rights.component: U-M 2023 revisions, transcription additions, and structural encoding
rights.basis: The item-level U-M XML states CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 Unported for this distribution.
The current Oxford repository record asserts CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 for the deposit.
notes: The license assertion is distinct from the separate copyright in Smetana's editorial contribution and does not by itself resolve commercial reuse of edition-derived readings.

rights.component: Smetana's 1977 editorial text, introduction, apparatus, comments, glossary, and other original editorial matter
rights.basis: The visible copyright page states copyright 1977 by the Pontifical Institute of Mediaeval Studies. United States Copyright Office Circular 15A states a 95-year term for protected pre-1978 works and automatic renewal for 1964–1977 publications, placing ordinary 1977 protection through the end of 2072.
notes: The date range assumes the ordinary validity of the displayed copyright notice. Public-domain status of Capgrave's medieval text does not remove copyright from sufficiently original modern editorial contributions.

rights.component: Huntington digital manuscript photography and IIIF derivatives
rights.basis: The IIIF manifest omits the license field and links no item-level open license. Huntington says no permission is required to quote or publish public-domain material or material whose copyright it does not own, requests precise citation, and leaves rights assessment to the researcher.
notes: The public-domain medieval text and public download availability do not establish a Creative Commons or unrestricted commercial license for the modern image files.

rights.component: DIMEV v1.0 dataset and catalog record
rights.basis: The DIMEV v1.0 release is supplied under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

rights.component: Sarah James's 2013 scholarly chapter and author accepted manuscript
rights.basis: The companion volume's copyright page states copyright contributors 2013, all rights reserved; the Kent repository record labels the licence UNSPECIFIED rather than granting an open reuse licence.
notes: Public access to the author accepted manuscript supports research consultation but does not itself authorize redistribution or republication.

rights.component: Gribbin's 2000 scholarly appendix and publisher presentation
rights.basis: The chapter is a modern scholarly contribution in a 2000 Boydell & Brewer book and Cambridge Core restricts full-text access; no open licence was found.
notes: Only the public bibliographic record and summary were used; no restricted chapter file was downloaded.

rights.basis: Circular 15A is an official work prepared and published by the United States Copyright Office, a federal government agency.
notes: Its public-domain status does not confer any rights in the copyrighted works whose duration it helps researchers assess.

editorial_practices.source_feature: OTA text with omitted lines, evident OCR artifacts, keyboard substitutions, and print-edition angle-bracketed emendations
editorial_practices.representation: Text checked against Smetana; about 200 lines keyed, obvious OCR errors repaired, proper thorn/yogh characters restored, and printed angle-bracketed emendations represented in square brackets
editorial_practices.description: U-M/MEC compared the OTA file with the 1977 print edition, restored roughly 200 omitted lines, repaired the most obvious OCR errors, converted keyboard kludges, and changed the visible delimiters around editorial emendations from angle to square brackets.
notes: The exact set of corrected readings is not enumerated in the header; users should collate any quotation-sensitive passage against Smetana and, where possible, HM 55.

editorial_practices.representation: A modern English editorial frame—two headings, opener, byline, dateline, and prose argument—followed by the Middle English poem with 37 chapter divisions, line numbering, printed page breaks, and manuscript folio milestones
editorial_practices.description: The encoding represents the poem plus explicit modern U-M project framing rather than the whole source book. Smetana's introduction, Peter J. Lucas's comments, glossary, bibliography, scriptural citations, and index are not encoded as content; the modern prose frame is representation-level paratext rather than part of Capgrave's medieval work.

editorial_practices.representation: Plain-text title and poem with embedded manuscript folio labels
editorial_practices.description: The surviving OTA bitstream contains the poem, not Smetana's introductory and reference matter. Its scattered line omissions appear accidental consequences of the capture, not a planned editorial excerpt.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, CME90162 XML, 2023 revision, publication-bound General Corpus source copy.
evidence.summary: Establishes mixed CME/OTA identifiers; the 13 February 2023 revision entry, 21 February 2023 header creation, and February 2023 publication/body dateline; U-M's CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 statement; the tentative OCR-from-Smetana provenance; the repair workflow; the modern prose editorial frame; all 37 chapter containers; printed-page and manuscript-folio references; and the self-dated ending in Assumption week 1440.

evidence.citation: General Corpus publication source overrides, reviewed 2026-07-14, CME90162 entry.
evidence.summary: Binds the approved CME90162 PDF and its SHA-256 to recent_changes/additions_2023/CME90162.xml and records the alternate CME_from_OTA path and both XML fixities.

evidence.citation: Byte comparison of the two CME90162 git blobs at Kyvero-Press/CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: source/recent_changes/additions_2023/CME90162.xml versus source/CME_from_OTA/CME90162.xml, from the EEBO start tag onward
evidence.summary: The current file (git blob 1edb3135f3483e18a9a3a5b7f992a81c3fad2b4d) and alternate file (git blob 56100072b883c0765aaebc68d5860c88abf9bcab) have different headers and whole-file fixities but byte-identical EEBO textual subtrees.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library persistent resolver for CME90162.
evidence.summary: Provides the canonical item-level U-M resolver encoded in the source identifiers.
notes: The automated request received an anti-bot response; bibliographic and textual claims are therefore grounded primarily in the exact U-M XML.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive, The life of St. John Norbert, repository record 20.500.14106/0162.
evidence.locator: Full item metadata and file inventory
evidence.summary: Identifies ota:0162, Capgrave, P. J. Lucas as contributor, the 1983 date, the public plain-text file, and the current CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 license assertion.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive, stnorbert0162.txt, exact public bitstream for deposit 0162.
evidence.summary: Preserves the original poem-only plain text, folio labels, legacy thorn/yogh substitutions, OCR/keying artifacts, and line omissions that U-M later revised.

evidence.citation: National Library of Ireland, The life of St. Norbert by John Capgrave, edited by Cyril Lawrence Smetana, catalog record vtls000062214.
evidence.summary: Confirms Capgrave's authorship, Smetana's editorship, the Toronto PIMS 1977 imprint, Studies and Texts 40, viii + 179 pages, ISBN 0888440405, ISSN 0082-5328, Middle English verse translation of a Latin Vita, and edition from HM 55.

evidence.citation: Google Books limited preview of John Capgrave, The Life of St. Norbert, ed. Cyril Lawrence Smetana (PIMS, 1977).
evidence.locator: PP5 title page; PP6 copyright/cataloging page; PP7 contents
evidence.summary: Direct inspection verifies the title and editor, the copyright 1977 PIMS notice, identifiers, and contents: introduction p. 1, Life of Saint Norbert / MS HM 55 p. 19, Comments by Peter J. Lucas p. 156, glossary p. 157, bibliography p. 171, scriptural citations p. 175, and index p. 177.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for lifeofstnorbert0000capg, Smetana's 1977 edition.
evidence.summary: Confirms the bibliographic identity and marks the item access-restricted-item true; complete scan derivatives are controlled-lending files rather than anonymous public downloads.

evidence.citation: The Huntington Library, mssHM 55, Capgrave's Life of St. Norbert, collection and catalog records.
evidence.locator: Object description, contents, physical description, provenance, access restriction, and image note
evidence.summary: Identifies HM 55 / Phillipps 24309, dates it [1440], calls it a presentation copy made by Capgrave and evidently autograph, identifies Capgrave's interlinear corrections and signature, maps the poem to ff. 1–59v, separates the lifted Latin breviary pastedown fragment, records quire and binding details, and explicitly labels the five Digital Scriptorium images a partial scan. The record does not independently claim that HM 55 is the sole witness.

evidence.citation: Huntington Digital Library IIIF Presentation manifest for mssHM 55 selected images.
evidence.locator: Five canvases: f. 1r; ff. 32v–33r; f. 59v; fore edge; inside back cover
evidence.summary: Verifies the exact five-canvas inventory, three work-overlapping text images, two binding views, Image API services, attribution, and omission of an item-level license field.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of all five cached Huntington HM 55 images and the exact IIIF inventory.
evidence.summary: Confirms legible full-color images with targets, the historiated presentation initial at f. 1r, the central sample spread, Capgrave's ending and Feliciter signature at f. 59v, and the two binding views. The inventory visibly lacks a continuous leaf sequence.

evidence.citation: The Huntington Library, Using the Library.
evidence.summary: Explains reader eligibility and application, Aeon requests, access to high-resolution HDL images, and research use of library collections.

evidence.citation: The Huntington Library, Aeon duplication and imaging request terms.
evidence.summary: Provides the reproduction-order route, states that fees and physical-condition or access restrictions apply, gives a standard turnaround of about 15 business days with extended handling for more than 50 images, and distinguishes delivery from the researcher's rights responsibility.

evidence.citation: The Huntington Library, Imaging Services Price List.
evidence.locator: Manuscript PDF scans and administrative fees
evidence.summary: Lists manuscript PDF scans at USD 1 per page for non-profit use and USD 2 per page for commercial use, directs orders through Aeon, and notes additional administrative fees for larger orders and that prices may change.
notes: The PDF was inspected through the official web endpoint but not cached after repeated automated download attempts returned HTTP 429.

evidence.citation: The Huntington Library, Library Rights and Permissions.
evidence.summary: States that the Library does not require permission to quote or publish public-domain material or material whose copyright it does not own, asks users to cite the exact item, and places copyright assessment and third-party permission responsibility on the researcher.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, live record 2973, The Life of St. Norbert.
evidence.summary: Confirms that the live DIMEV record number is 2973 and the legacy IMEV/NIMEV number is 1805. The latter must not be used as recID because recID=1805 identifies an unrelated poem.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, v1.0 release, Zenodo record 14585961.
evidence.summary: Records Capgrave, Life of St. Norbert, opening Joy and grace peace love faith and charity, saints' life and envoy subjects, seven-line ababbcc rhyme royal, unique HM 55 witness ff. 1–59v, Feliciter signature, Smetana 1977, Horstmann 1893 extracts, and Norfolk witness localization. The release is CC BY 4.0.

evidence.citation: Sarah James, “Capgrave, Bokenham, and Late Medieval Saints' Lives,” in A Companion to Fifteenth-Century English Poetry, ed. Julia Boffey and A. S. G. Edwards (Cambridge: D. S. Brewer, 2013), 99–112, author accepted manuscript, ISBN 9781843843535.
evidence.summary: Describes the poem as a 4,109-line rhyme-royal life completed in 1440 and dedicated to John Wygenhale of West Dereham, closely translating an early Latin Vita while adding and omitting material and framing the work through prologue and envoy.
notes: The official repository exposed the exact PDF but its TLS chain was self-signed; the file was retrieved with certificate verification disabled only after the landing page established the exact official URL. Exact bytes were preserved. qpdf reported a non-fatal object-count warning; page extraction and text inspection succeeded for all 17 pages.

The repository labels the licence UNSPECIFIED, and the volume copyright page states copyright contributors 2013, all rights reserved. The chapter does not identify HM 55 or support the pre-1422 composition proposal.

evidence.citation: Joseph A. Gribbin, “The Date of John Capgrave's Life of St Norbert,” appendix in The Premonstratensian Order in Late Medieval England (Woodbridge: Boydell & Brewer, 2000), 248–250.
evidence.locator: Publisher chapter summary and bibliographic metadata
evidence.summary: The official publisher summary attributes to Peter J. Lucas the proposal that Capgrave began the work before 1422 for an unnamed prior abbot and completed and dedicated it in 1440; it presents evidence and historical argument rather than a settled alternative date.
notes: The full chapter is access restricted. No restricted file was downloaded, and a separate publicly available book preview containing only front matter was not treated as evidence for this appendix.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Duration of Copyright, Circular 15A, revised April 2026.
evidence.locator: Pre-1978 publication terms and automatic renewal for works published 1964–1977
evidence.summary: States the 95-year maximum term for protected works published before 1978 and automatic renewal for works published in 1964–1977, supporting the ordinary United States term calculation for a copyright-noticed 1977 edition.
notes: Exact official bytes were preserved. qpdf reported non-fatal linearization hint-table warnings; the file is unencrypted and readable, so it was not rewritten or repaired.

open_questions.question: Which exact Latin Vita Sancti Norberti recension and physical exemplar did Capgrave translate?
next_steps: Consult Smetana's full introduction and later Capgrave source studies for a recension-level identification.
Compare Capgrave's additions, omissions, and chapter sequence against the BHL 6248–6250 Vita tradition without assigning any one BHL number until direct evidence supports it.
If an exemplar is proposed, verify whether the claim names a physical witness, an abstract recension, or only a modern edition.
notes: The present manifest records a generic translation relationship in work metadata but does not create an unproved physical-source edge.

open_questions.question: Was the poem wholly composed in 1440, or does the completed presentation text revise material begun before 1422?
next_steps: Read Gribbin's full appendix, the underlying Peter J. Lucas chronology argument, and subsequent responses rather than treating the publisher summary as the complete case.
Keep the secure Assumption-week 1440 completion separate from any proposed earlier compositional phase.

open_questions.question: What exact OCR, keying, compilation, and checking workflow produced OTA 0162, and what was P. J. Lucas's role?
next_steps: Ask the Oxford Text Archive for legacy accession documentation and production notes for ota:0162.
Compare the full OTA text against Smetana's pages and apparatus to enumerate omissions and transformations rather than relying only on the U-M summary.
Clarify whether the deposit's compiled by credit refers to OCR preparation, text correction, or another editorial contribution.

open_questions.question: Can a complete authorized color surrogate of physical HM 55 be obtained, including every textual leaf, the lifted former pastedown fragment, blanks, endleaves, and all binding surfaces?
next_steps: Submit a Huntington Aeon reproduction request with an explicit whole-object shot list and ask whether complete existing master photography is available.

Ask whether HM 55's restricted physical status permits full imaging and whether a non-profit or commercial rate applies to this repository's intended research and possible downstream commercial editions.
On delivery, retain the complete lawful object in source-cache and map the work narrowly to ff. 1r–59v while keeping the Latin former pastedown fragment separate.
notes: Only the five-image Digital Scriptorium selection was publicly accessible on the review date.

open_questions.question: How do the OTA/U-M CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 licenses, Smetana's still-protected editorial contribution, and any Huntington delivery terms constrain commercial self-publishing derived from this source chain?
next_steps: Seek written clarification from Oxford/U-M about the intended scope and licensable rights behind the item-level CC license, especially for commercial reuse.
Ask PIMS for permission or a scope clarification before reproducing or closely deriving protected Smetana editorial matter commercially.
Review the exact terms accompanying any Huntington reproduction delivery before sharing files or using images in publication.
Keep research use of source formatting distinct from copying protected editorial expression or redistributing modern facsimile files.
